title: Brookline runner takes on first Boston Marathon for One World Strong
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Brookline resident Elena Castellini, 23, is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026 to support One World Strong Foundation.
One World Strong Foundation, founded by 2013 bomb survivor Dave Fortier, aids trauma survivors through resilience-building community programs.
The foundation offers medical help and psychological support, including prosthetics, to victims in Ukraine and beyond.

title: South Boston woman fatally stabbed boyfriend during an argument, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, from South Boston, was charged with manslaughter after allegedly stabbing her boyfriend, Kyle Bradford, 26.
Police responded to 258 Old Colony Ave. where they found Bradford with a stab wound; he later died at the hospital.
Pascual, held on $100,000 bond, claims self-defense, citing prior threats and physical assault from Bradford.

title: Patriots to be featured on HBO’s ‘Hard Knocks’ ahead of 2027 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots will be featured on HBO's 'Hard Knocks' in 2027 for the first time.
The Seattle Seahawks will be on 'Hard Knocks' ahead of the 2026 NFL season.
NFL eligibility rules now allow more playoff teams on 'Hard Knocks', prompting the Patriots' participation.

title: James Hagens notches first points with Providence Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens scored his first professional goal in a 6-4 Providence Bruins win against the Bridgeport Islanders.
Hagens recorded a goal and an assist in back-to-back AHL games, aiding Providence's recent performances.
Hagens is on an AHL amateur tryout contract and may join the Boston Bruins if he signs an entry-level contract.

title: Could Patriots circle back with Stefon Diggs? Here’s what Eliot Wolf said of a potential reunion.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots released Stefon Diggs due to a $26.8 million cap hit, but haven't ruled out a cheaper reunion.
Eliot Wolf stated the door isn't closed on re-signing Diggs, noting potential scenarios like injuries or scheme changes.
Diggs led the Patriots in 2025 with 85 catches for 1,013 yards but had poor postseason performance.

title: Man sentenced for raping 2 women in Framingham store in 1989
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, sentenced to at least 50 years for raping two women in a Framingham store in 1989.
Gale convicted of four counts of aggravated rape, two counts of kidnapping, and one count of armed robbery.
DNA evidence and investigative genetic genealogy led to Gale's identification in the decades-old cold case.

title: Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla on possibility of winning coach of the year: ‘It’s a stupid award’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla considers the NBA Coach of the Year award 'stupid,' emphasizing credit should go to players and staff.
Celtics have the fourth-best NBA record despite Jayson Tatum's significant absence this season.
Under Mazzulla, the Celtics secured a fifth-consecutive 50-win season and are second in the Eastern Conference.

title: 1 dead after crash in Mattapan, police say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: One person was killed in a car crash on American Legion Highway in Mattapan late Sunday night, police reported.
The crash occurred at the intersection of Walk Hill Street and Canterbury Street around 11:14 p.m., according to police.
The victim was found inside the car and pronounced dead at the scene, with significant damage reported to the vehicle.

title: Should Joe Mazzulla win Coach of the Year? Here’s how he stacks up against the competition.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla is a leading candidate for NBA Coach of the Year with the second-best odds at +155.
J.B. Bickerstaff of the Detroit Pistons is the favorite to win, with his team holding a 54-20 record.
The Celtics, under Mazzulla, maintain the second-best record in the East despite significant player turnover.

title: Ice sculpture carved for Boston’s ‘No Kings’ rally destroyed by vandals, organizers say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Ice sculpture reading 'End ICE' destroyed by vandals after 'No Kings' protest in Boston Common.
Artist Kat Dressler designed the sculpture to maintain its message even if vandalized.
Boston police did not file a report despite being dispatched to the scene of the vandalism.
